name: CodeScribe
title: CodeScribe — AGENT card

what_this_repo_is.summary: CodeScribe is an AI-assisted framework for scientific software development.
capabilities: Incremental Fortran → C++ translation (with interface layers)
what_this_repo_is.usage_note: Use this repo when you need automated, repo-aware code changes with an auditable tool loop.

model_setup.supports: OpenAI-compatible backends via the `oaic-` model prefix.

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_BASEURL
environment_variables.description: Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible API (must include /v1).

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_PROVIDER
environment_variables.description: Provider label (used internally for auth routing).

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_APIKEY
environment_variables.description: Required by the current implementation (even if the endpoint itself needs no auth).

model_setup.example_command: code-scribe inspect README.rst -m oaic-llama3.1 -q "Summarize this project and list key entry points."

discover_capabilities.description: CodeScribe workflows are exposed as CLI subcommands.

bounded_vs_unbounded.description: Runs repeated fresh sessions over a task file and uses bounded tools rooted at the working tree. This reduces risk from arbitrary shell commands and path escapes.
Runs a single tool-using session. Useful for interactive, one-off tasks and may be less restricted than loop mode.

notes: Bounded mode is a practical constraint layer, not an OS sandbox.

minimal_workflows.name: Single agent session
minimal_workflows.command: code-scribe agent "Find failing tests and propose a minimal fix." -m oaic-llama3.1

minimal_workflows.name: Bounded loop over a task file

considerations: Agent runs are capped by an iteration limit; tasks may stop early if not completed.
Bounded `bash` is allowlisted and rejects shell metacharacters; some commands will fail by design.
Prefer `edit` for precise changes to existing files; use `write` for new/fully replaced files.
